Tests the `charCodeAt` method of the `String` object to verify it returns the correct character code.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the functionality of the `charCodeAt` method in JavaScript. It checks if calling `charCodeAt(0)` on the string `"abc"` returns the Unicode value `97`, which corresponds to the character `'a'`. The use of `assert` ensures that the test will throw an error if the condition is not met, indicating a failure in the expected behavior of the `charCodeAt` method. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of string manipulation in JavaScript.
